setting.note: A single evening in Lahore frames the whole book: a Pakistani man buttonholes an uneasy American at a cafe in the Old Anarkali bazaar and talks him through his life over tea and a long dinner, the two of them drifting out toward the Mall as the light goes. His memories carry the story abroad, to Princeton, Manhattan, Manila and Valparaiso, but the ground under the telling stays fixed in Lahore. The map holds the old bazaar quarter and the colonial spine of the Mall, with the outside chapters pinned where they belong.

palette.note: The deep green of the Mughal gardens and tilework Lahore keeps at its heart, and of the flag the narrator returns to. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
